chunk_summary: Counsel families on safe milk handling, donor milk benefits, fortification, and avoidance of informal milk sharing to protect infant health, especially in NICU settings.
learning_objectives: Understand safe practices for handling expressed milk, including hygiene, storage, and thawing.
Explain the benefits and safety protocols of donor milk, including NEC prevention and pasteurization.
Discourage informal milk sharing and promote regulated alternatives like milk banking.
Educate families on fortification and safe formula preparation for preterm infants.
key_facts: Donor milk is recommended for NEC prevention in very preterm infants.
Fortifiers are often bovine-derived and require sterile preparation to avoid contamination.
Informal milk sharing poses risks due to incomplete screening, adulteration, and cold-chain issues.
In India, donor milk is provided through regulated donation models, not commercial sale.
Safe formula preparation is preferred over unsafe substitutes like animal milk or diluted feeds.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Strongly discourage informal or online milk sharing and unregulated wet-nursing because screening is incomplete, milk may be adulterated, and cold-chain integrity is unknown. (Source: FDA - https://www.fda.gov/science-research/pediatrics/usedonorhumanmilk)
document_key_topics: Benefits and protocols of donor milk
Fortification and feeding alternatives for preterm infants
